key_insight: Any FEM-like multiscale basis with fixed locality and fixed coefficient visibility cannot guarantee vanishing error uniformly over all bounded-contrast rough coefficients; its worst-case error stays bounded away from zero.
engineering_relevance: To achieve robust multiscale accuracy in practice, scalable methods must relax locality or use broader coefficient information as the mesh is refined.
method: Formalizes a notion of structural scalability for local multiscale bases.
Proves an order-one lower bound for deterministic constructions with fixed support radius, information radius, and local multiplicity.
Uses periodic coefficient families, corrector approximation lower bounds, and mesh-density arguments to build the counterexample.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a worst-case Galerkin error lower bound that remains positive and independent of H, established on a fixed finite family of smooth periodic coefficients and right-hand sides.
watch_item: The negative result applies to deterministic methods with fixed locality/visibility bounds, so practical schemes may still succeed if they increase patch size, spectral dimension, or coefficient access.
paper_title: An Order-One Lower Bound on the Error of Scalable Generalized Multiscale Finite Element Space Constructions
